transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Good afternoon to you.
Good morning.
How's SF?
It's good.
I was supposed to fly out tomorrow, but our government is in a disaster currently.
And apparently Trump is threatening to cancel, to like shut down.
air traffic control on Friday and like all the major cities.
So I had to change my flight today.
Oh no.
Yeah, I saw some stuff on Instagram.
There were like huge lines for TSA.
Yeah, I know.
It's already delayed.
Hi, Marylise.
Good morning.
How are you?
Good.
How was the trick-or-treating last Friday since that's the last time we talked to you?
it was really still getting through your candy?
Yeah, it was fun.
Actually, candies were not in too high volume, but it was a lot of people.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
How old is your little one?
They're 8 and 11.
Okay, yeah.
I was trick-or-treating with like four-year-olds and so they like, it was like five houses and then everybody got bored because their attention spans are so short.
I didn't know if you guys like had a longer journey than I did.
how sounds you
We had a fairly long, but my little one wanted to get home.
was, I think, tired and getting grumpy.
And I think he was scared because there was just too many people.
Oh, yeah.
Yeah.
Okay.
Well, we got your email yesterday.
Tyler forwarded that.
So, you know, totally understand.
Yeah.
I mean, I just wanted to log in and tell the team, like, it's not you guys.
I think it's just the model that you guys work with that I just, we have come to realize probably doesn't work for BaseTen.
There's so much AI in the process, which is kind of interesting for an AI company.
I don't but yeah.
Yeah.
Okay.
Yeah.
I mean, that makes a lot of sense.
think, like, we aim for volume and to just, like, the, just search results and getting picked up.
And if you're thinking about, like, human readers who are highly technical, there's definitely still a gap there in the AI delivering on that front.
But in terms of us.
Yeah.
Like for the next week, we have three more outlines that we're finalizing.
I don't know if you, like, we're happy to send those over, but also like knowing that we're sort of not meeting the bar, I don't know how valuable they would be to you.
Yeah.
So why is your take on that?
Yeah, I mean, I think if you're close, like, why don't you send them?
I was also just thinking about, you know, all the list of ideas and articles.
We have to figure out how we're going to get this done.
Like, it's still like a question mark to me because I still need to address the issue not being indexed by Elena improperly.
Yeah.
So we just, we just need to, like, I need to sit down with Mike and see, like, what we're going to do next.
So I think it just gave us, like, a lot of great ideas.
It's just, like, how are going to format the content is, like, the big question.
Yeah.
Okay.
Yeah.
So I'd love to get the outlines because I think we might be able to use them in the future.
Yeah.
Perfect.
Yeah.
We can send the outlines.
We'll give you guys access and we can.
And you can export, or we can export the content OS for you with all of those ideas.
That would be great.
Yeah, I was wondering if I can export that.
Yeah.

Yeah, and you'll, of course, have access to all of the artifacts that we generated, which, like, we do feed into, like, all the LLMs that we, like, they're in our own, like, platform, proprietary platform.
But also, like, if we're doing post-processing and clawed or perplexity, like, those are fed, so they will be useful if you do end up, like, scaling up an AI in another fashion.
Yeah.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, I mean, you know, it's interesting.
I've been thinking about this whole thing and, like, how you guys are going to work.
Because I think I see more and more, more and more, like, erroneous information for LLMs, you know?
I mean, we use them, obviously.
And, like, we have a lot of knowledge and stuff.
We'll correct it.
But I'm just wondering how the market is going to evolve, because it looks like there's just a lot of, like, wrong information out there.
Yeah.
I mean, just to, like, give you...
I don't think we ever showed you what, like, the platform looks like on the back end, but just so that you can kind of see the steps that go into it.
So this is one of the agentic pipelines that we built for you guys and some of the pieces that, like, just the topics that we put in.
And if you look at kind of all of the steps that this goes through, we're generating the brief, then we're doing, like, deep research.
And throughout each of these pieces, that's the output, but you can kind of, like, these are the inputs that we're putting in.
You know, we're looking at all of the parameters, looking at your personas, pulling all of those in.
And then, you know, source links, we're validating the writing guidelines, you know, meta tags, and then doing a fact checking layer.
So there is a lot going into this, and we're constantly, like, adding.
And tweaking like the, you know, if you're looking at the code, you know, this is the output, sorry.
That's, you know, this is all going in and out and we can like tweak this on the back end a little bit.
But yeah, there's a lot of things that we're constantly like pulling apart and trying to add different steps to and play with.
So it is very, there's a lot going into it, but still like so much more that you just like can't control the exact output.
Yeah.
I mean, you get, you get, you like, you feel like as much as you can to them.
It's just like, you know, you can't control what they have been trained on and all this stuff, unfortunately.
And I think that's, that's what we're running into at this point.
So yeah, yeah, totally fair.
Okay, well, we can send all of that.
Is there anything else that you want or, or like that we can help with over the next week to close this out?
yeah, getting all the export because I think I'm, you know, to build.
want

I it, whatever strategy I'm going to come up with to figure it out, like, I would definitely want to look at it, because I think all the outlines, you know, I was excited about it, because I think they were, they were great.
I mean, not the outlines, the topics, and I was like, yeah, it's all good stuff.
So I'm kind of really bummed.
I mean, I like to read the so it has to end, but yeah.
Yeah, well, you know, we will stay in touch.
I think this product, like, there's a world in 2026 where we start to surface kind of the back end.
To clients, and like, maybe you guys could play around with it, and have the sort of expertise to put in, like, the exact prompts and exact directions, that maybe you can get those results.
So we'll stay in touch, and obviously, you'll have visibility into what comes next.
Cool, yeah.
I think it depends on how scale the team, too.
I think the current people, like, working, they're already overloaded.
So when I ask them to, like, check and write, like, nobody has time.
And so, yeah.
But we will have to figure this out.
Cool.
Yeah.
Right.
Well, thank you so much.
for listening.
Yeah, thank you.
For everything, you know, no bad reflection on the team, really.
I really enjoyed it.
So I wanted to let you know.
Likewise.
Yeah.
Thank you, Marylise.
And we'll talk to you soon.
Okay.
Thanks.
Thank you.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Finalize the Baseten \<\> GrowthX partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=131.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Baseten is ending the partnership; GrowthX's AI-driven model cannot produce the deep, technical content required for Baseten's audience.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=131.0)
  - [GrowthX will deliver three final article outlines and export all project artifacts (outlines, ideas, research) for Baseten's future use.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
  - [Baseten will regroup internally to define a new content strategy, as the core problem of low search indexability remains unresolved.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=202.0)

## Topics

### Partnership Conclusion

  - [Baseten is ending the partnership, as GrowthX's AI-driven model is a poor fit for the required content quality.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=131.0)
  - [**Rationale:** The model's focus on search volume over technical depth fails to meet the high standards of Baseten's expert audience.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=144.0)
  - [**Context:** This decision is not a reflection on the GrowthX team, but a mismatch in methodologies.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)

### GrowthX's AI Pipeline

  - [Andi demonstrated GrowthX's agentic pipeline to show the rigor applied, despite the output mismatch.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=298.0)
  - [**Process:** The pipeline uses multiple LLMs (e.g., Claude, Perplexity) and includes steps for:](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=298.0)
      - [Brief generation](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=308.0)
      - [Deep research](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=319.0)
      - [Persona & guideline validation](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=330.0)
      - [Fact-checking](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=346.0)
  - [**Constraint:** Even with this process, the final output quality is limited by the inherent unpredictability of LLM training data.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=395.0)

### Project Handoff & Future Strategy

  - [Baseten will use all project artifacts to inform its new content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=229.0)
  - [**Deliverables:**](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
      - [Three final article outlines](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
      - [Export of the "Content OS" (all ideas, outlines, research)](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=236.0)
  - [**Baseten's Path Forward:**](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=202.0)
      - [The core problem of low search indexability remains.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=202.0)
      - [Marylise will meet with Mike to define a new strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=202.0)
      - [**Challenge:** The current team is at capacity, making internal content creation difficult.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=465.0)
  - [**Future Collaboration:** Andi suggested a potential future partnership (e.g., 2026) if GrowthX offers clients direct access to its backend platform, enabling Baseten to apply its own expertise to guide the AI.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=440.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
      - [Send the three final article outlines.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
      - [Export and deliver the "Content OS" with all project artifacts.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=236.0)
  - [**Baseten:**](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=202.0)
      - [Meet internally (Marylise & Mike) to define a new content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/QdtWwFaxcGrD-nksagKE8GCpexdkDP4_?tab=summary&timestamp=202.0)

action_items.description: Email Marylise 3 outlines + Content OS export; grant platform access
Meet Mike re: content strategy/format + indexing; use GrowthX exports
